name: Causal Language Modeling
oneliner: The training objective where a model predicts the next token using only the tokens to its left — the foundation of GPT-style generation.
explanation: Causal language modeling trains a model to predict each token given all preceding tokens, never peeking at future positions. A causal attention mask ensures position i can only attend to positions 0 through i. This is the pre-training objective behind GPT-2, GPT-3, GPT-4, LLaMA, and Mistral. At inference time the same left-to-right constraint enables autoregressive generation: sample one token, append it, repeat. Because every position provides a training signal, causal LM is highly data-efficient compared to masked LM approaches.
fundamentals: The causal LM objective maximises $\mathcal{L} = \sum_{t=1}^{T} \log P(x_t \mid x_{<t}; \theta)$. The causal mask is a lower-triangular binary matrix $M$ where $M_{ij} = 1$ iff $j \leq i$. Perplexity $= \exp(-\mathcal{L}/T)$ is the standard evaluation metric.
